name: William Gouge
title: Puritan Pastor & Westminster Divine
bio: English Puritan pastor who served at St. Ann Blackfriars in London for 46 years and was a member of the Westminster Assembly. His 'Of Domesticall Duties' was one of the most comprehensive Puritan treatments of family life, and his commentary on Hebrews remained influential for generations.
faith: Gouge was known for his consistent pastoral faithfulness — nearly half a century at one church. As a Westminster divine, he helped craft the Westminster Confession of Faith and Catechisms. His practical theology of household piety shaped Puritan family life across England and New England.
tagline: 46 years of faithful pastoral ministry — Westminster divine
